message:
refactor(gateway): eliminate non-null assertions with proper null checks

Systematic removal of TypeScript non-null assertions (!) across gateway
services and handlers, replacing them with proper null/undefined checks
for better compile-time safety.

Pattern changes:
- `rows[0]!` → `const row = rows[0]; if (!row) return;`
- `array[i]!` → `const item = array[i]; if (item !== undefined) ...`
- `value!.property` → `if (value) value.property`

Affected services:
- budget, safety, approval, cost-forecast, cost-optimization
- dcg-cli, dcg-config, dcg-pending, dcg-ru-integration, dcg-stats, dcg
- git-conflict, git-sync, git, graph-bridge, handoff, history
- metrics, notification, rationale-generator, reservation
- ru-fleet, ru-sweep, ru-sync, safety-rules.engine, supervisor
- agent-detection, alerts

Also updated:
- Job handlers: codebase-scan, context-build
- Web: websocket-context

These changes ensure TypeScript catches potential null/undefined access
at compile time rather than relying on runtime assertions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
